last_completed_round.direction_name: Keep The Initial Prime Retime But Restore The Future Refill To B-Then-A
last_completed_round.hypothesis: Round 16/17 showed that the full prefetch retime improved the narrower refill-only variant from 25.67678452 ms down to 25.62406445 ms, but it still missed the accepted export base at 24.84582424 ms. That points to a mixed result inside the family: the initial A-then-B prime likely helps, while carrying A-then-B all the way into the future refill likely over-rotates and reintroduces barrier cost. The only remaining untested permutation in this family is the hybrid ordering where the initial two prime stages stay A-then-B but the steady-state future refill goes back to B-then-A, which also matches the nearby 128x128 PTX sibling path.
